title: Perfectoid Spaces — Scholze Program Proof DAG

lean_angle.scope: Formalized the *definition* of perfectoid space (1111.4914 §3) — not the theorems
lean_angle.comparison: Our DAG covers the theorem cluster Lean has not reached. Direct 'definition formalized, theorem chain not formalized' contrast.

1111.4914.title: Perfectoid spaces
1111.4914.role: root — foundational definitions, tilting equivalence, almost purity, weight-monodromy in special cases

1211.6357.title: Moduli of p-divisible groups
1211.6357.role: direct successor — perfectoid Shimura varieties, moduli space at infinite level

1602.03148.title: Integral p-adic Hodge theory
1602.03148.role: BMS1 — integrates perfectoid technology with crystalline / de Rham comparison

1802.03261.title: Topological Hochschild homology and integral p-adic Hodge theory
1802.03261.role: BMS2 — recasts BMS1 via THH; bridge to homotopy theory

1905.08229.title: Prisms and prismatic cohomology
1905.08229.role: successor / unification — prismatic site subsumes crystalline, etale, de Rham

1709.07343.title: Étale cohomology of diamonds
1709.07343.role: framework-generalization — diamonds as v-sheaves of perfectoid spaces

1301.0792.title: Relative p-adic Hodge theory: Foundations
1301.0792.role: parallel-foundation — independent development of relative perfectoid-style theory

2102.13459.title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence
2102.13459.role: lateral-application — diamonds power a geometric Langlands construction

1907.10525.title: Prismatic Dieudonné theory
1907.10525.role: application — antiequivalence between $p$-divisible groups and prismatic Dieudonné modules; extends Scholze–Weinstein classification

2005.04746.title: Prismatization
2005.04746.role: alternative-engine — geometric / moduli-stack reformulation of prismatic cohomology via $\Sigma'$

2201.06120.title: Absolute prismatic cohomology
2201.06120.role: extension — absolute (vs relative) prismatic cohomology via the Cartier-Witt stack

related_dags.langlands-local: Shares the node 2102.13459 (Fargues–Scholze, Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence). Here that paper is the application terminus of the perfectoid / p-adic Hodge machinery (it imports étale cohomology of diamonds 1709.07343 and the p-adic Hodge inputs); in the langlands-local DAG it is the ROOT whose construction the downstream papers verify, refine, and extend. The shared node is the load-bearing handoff from p-adic geometry into the Langlands program.

Faltings:1988.title: p-adic Hodge theory
Faltings:1988.role: foundational — almost mathematics, almost étale extensions

FontaineWintenberger:1979.title: Le "corps des normes" de certaines extensions algébriques de corps locaux
FontaineWintenberger:1979.role: foundational — classical predecessor of the tilting equivalence (char 0 ↔ char p)

Huber:1996.title: Étale Cohomology of Rigid Analytic Varieties and Adic Spaces
Huber:1996.role: foundational — adic spaces, the geometric category perfectoid spaces live inside

Faltings:2002.title: Almost étale extensions
Faltings:2002.role: foundational — almost ring theory, used pervasively in 1111.4914

GabberRamero:2003.title: Almost ring theory
GabberRamero:2003.role: foundational — systematic treatment of almost mathematics
